name: TriNet HR Platform API
what_it_does: TriNet HR Platform REST API for small and medium businesses to integrate with TriNet's professional employer organization (PEO) services covering payroll, benefits administration, HR compliance, and risk management. Enables AI agents to manage employee data and HR information system integration for workforce management automation, handle payroll processing and payroll change submission for automated payroll management, access benefits enrollment and administration for employee benefits automation, retrieve time and attendance data for payroll input automation, manage new hire onboarding and I-9 verification for employee onboarding automation, handle PTO and leave management for workforce scheduling automation, access compliance alerts and HR regulatory notifications for HR compliance automation, retrieve workers' compensation and risk management data for safety program automation, manage performance management data for HR analytics automation, and integrate TriNet with HRIS, accounting, and workforce management platforms for end-to-end PEO HR data synchronization.
use_cases: Syncing employee data from HR system integration agents
Submitting payroll changes from payroll management agents
Enrolling benefits from employee onboarding automation agents
Tracking PTO from workforce management agents
not_for: Enterprise Fortune 500 HR (TriNet targets SMB under 500 employees)
International payroll in non-TriNet supported countries
Standalone HR software without PEO relationship
best_when: A small or medium business using TriNet PEO services wanting AI agents to automate HR data synchronization, payroll change management, benefits administration, and compliance monitoring within the TriNet HR platform.
avoid_when: PEO CO-EMPLOYMENT LEGAL COMPLEXITY FOR AUTOMATED ACTIONS: TriNet PEO creates co-employment relationship where TriNet is employer of record for employment law purposes; automated HR actions via TriNet API have legal implications under co-employment structure; automated termination or pay changes without understanding co-employment legal responsibility can create liability under PEO agreement terms. IRS EMPLOYER IDENTIFICATION NUMBER TAX REPORTING: TriNet PEO files employment taxes under TriNet's EIN as employer of record; automated payroll changes must be processed through TriNet to maintain correct tax reporting; direct payroll processing outside TriNet creates tax reporting conflict with PEO co-employment structure. WORKERS' COMPENSATION CLASSIFICATION FOR AUTOMATED HEADCOUNT CHANGES: TriNet workers' comp coverage applies job classification codes; automated hiring workflows must include correct job classification for workers' comp rate assignment; automated new hire without job classification creates default classification that may misrepresent workers' comp risk and affect premium.

auth.notes: TriNet uses OAuth 2.0 for API access. REST API with JSON. Dublin, California HQ. Founded 1988. Public (NYSE: TNET). 16,000+ SMB clients. 350,000+ worksite employees. PEO (Professional Employer Organization) model — co-employment relationship. Products: HR, payroll, benefits, risk/insurance, HR compliance. Competes with Justworks, Insperity, and ADP TotalSource for SMB PEO services; Gusto and Rippling for tech-forward SMB HR platform.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-employee monthly fee
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-worksite employee monthly fee; varies by industry, location, and benefits mix; minimum employee count requirements
pricing.notes: Dublin CA. Public (NYSE: TNET). Per-employee monthly PEO fee. SMB target. No free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; HR and payroll queries throttled; webhooks for employee lifecycle and payroll events

known_agent_gotchas: PAYROLL CUTOFF DEADLINE FOR AUTOMATED PAYROLL CHANGES: TriNet payroll processing has submission cutoff deadlines (typically 4 business days before pay date for direct deposit); automated payroll changes submitted after cutoff process in following payroll cycle; automated same-pay-period change workflows must respect TriNet payroll cutoff calendar

BENEFITS OPEN ENROLLMENT WINDOW RESTRICTIONS: TriNet benefits changes are restricted to open enrollment windows except for qualifying life events (QLE); automated benefits change workflows must verify open enrollment status or QLE documentation before benefits change submission; automated benefits changes outside enrollment window without QLE are rejected

CO-EMPLOYMENT TERMINATION NOTICE REQUIREMENTS: TriNet PEO termination requires coordination between client company and TriNet; automated employee termination via TriNet API must follow PEO termination procedure including final pay compliance for each state; automated termination without state-specific final pay compliance (California 72-hour rule, etc.) creates labor law violation under co-employment

STATE TAX NEXUS FROM REMOTE EMPLOYEE ONBOARDING: TriNet handles multi-state payroll tax; automated remote employee onboarding in new states creates state tax nexus for the client company; automated hiring in new states without nexus awareness creates state tax registration obligation that must be coordinated with finance team

EMPLOYEE PII DATA ACCESS SCOPE RESTRICTION: TriNet API access to employee PII must comply with HR data minimum necessary access; automated HR integrations should request only necessary employee data scopes; broad PII access for automated workflows creates privacy risk for sensitive compensation and benefits data
agent_readiness.security_notes: PEO HR platform. SOC2, HIPAA. OAuth2. US. Employee HR, payroll, and benefits PII data.

security_score_components.security_notes: PEO HR. SOC2, HIPAA. OAuth2. US. Employee payroll, benefits, and HR PII data.
